description_long: This object consists of three spherical, olive-drab green metal casings connected by a central cylindrical mechanism, resembling a cluster of hand grenades or a multi-charge explosive device. Each sphere features a visible safety lever and a metal pull ring, with yellow bands around the necks indicating a possible arming or detonation mechanism. The surfaces are slightly rough and matte, with minor scuffs and wear, suggesting use or handling. The central connecting piece is darker and more industrial, possibly housing the main triggering mechanism. The object is compact, with all components tightly grouped, and the safety pins and levers are prominent and interactable. No visible digital or analog timer is present, and there are no clear warning labels or hazard symbols.
description: A cluster of three olive-green spherical bombs joined by a central mechanism, each with a safety lever and pull ring, featuring yellow neck bands and a rugged, matte finish.
description_view_0: Three spherical bombs are visible with prominent safety levers and pull rings, and a detached lever and ring are seen in the foreground.
description_view_1: The view emphasizes the connection between the spheres and the central mechanism, with the levers and rings more closely grouped.
description_view_2: The rear of the object shows the backs of the spheres and the central connector, with less detail on the safety mechanisms.
description_view_3: The yellow neck bands and the arrangement of the safety levers and rings are most visible, with a detached lever and ring lying nearby.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: Detonation appears to be initiated by pulling the metal ring to release the safety lever, which likely triggers an internal fuse or primer.
